prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on a black screen with an Alfa Romeo logo centered. Beneath it is the Italian line “La meccanica delle emozioni,” with very small legal-style text at the bottom that is not fully legible.

It then cuts abruptly to a different setting and brand context: a close-up of an adult woman with shoulder-length dark hair wearing a floral sleeveless blouse. She is indoors, with a softly blurred garage or home-interior background behind her.

The next shot widens to show the same woman standing in the opening of a garage framed by green ivy. Inside the garage are visible household and recreational items, including a bicycle and shelves. She raises a hand in a small wave. Large white text across the center reads “noom stories.”

The commercial then shifts to an outdoor poolside setting. A young adult woman with glasses and long reddish-purple hair stands beside a swimming pool. She wears a purple sleeveless crop top and dark patterned shorts. Behind her are red circular patio chairs, greenery, and hedges. A small white “noom” watermark appears in the upper-right corner, and a lower-third label identifies her as a Noom user, though the name is partly difficult to read in the sampled frame.

The clip cuts back to the first woman in the ivy-framed garage. A lower-third identifies her as “Katelyn” and “Noom User.” She stands facing the camera, gesturing with her hands. The “noom” watermark remains in the upper-right corner. A closer shot follows, showing her from the chest up against the blurred garage background.

The poolside woman reappears in a similar wide shot, still standing near the pool and red chairs, facing the camera.

The next setting is a bright white kitchen. An adult man with dark hair and a beard stands near a kitchen island, wearing a dark short-sleeved shirt. Behind him are white cabinets and a refrigerator covered with small photos or notes. A lower-third label reads “Mike” and “Noom User.” The “noom” watermark remains in the upper-right corner.

The commercial then cuts to a bright bedroom. An adult woman with long straight blonde hair sits or stands close to camera in front of a softly lit bed with white bedding. A lower-third label reads “Sarah” and “Noom User.” In the next wider shot, she stands smiling in front of the bed, wearing a patterned blouse and pink pants. The room has sloped ceilings, bedside tables, and a light, airy color palette.

The man in the kitchen appears again, first in a medium shot and then in a closer shot. The refrigerator photos are more prominent in the background. Small white subtitle-like text appears along the bottom in several shots, but it is too small and blurred to read clearly from the sampled frames.

The poolside woman appears again in a wide shot and then in a closer shot, smiling toward the camera with greenery behind her. The “noom” watermark remains visible in the upper-right corner.

Near the end, the video transitions to a solid coral-red background. Small white dotted elements appear briefly as part of a graphic animation. The final frame shows the white dotted “noom” logo centered, with the tagline “Change your mind.” underneath, and “noom.com” below that.
